title: Bad Credit Mortgages — Bruised Credit & Rebuild Programs
metaTitle: Bad Credit Mortgage in Canada — B-Lender & Credit Union Options
metaDescription: Bruised credit mortgage programs across Canada. Equifax 580–640 range options through B-lenders, credit unions and private financing. Pathway back to A-lender pricing.
intro: A 580 credit score doesn't mean you can't get a mortgage. It means you need a different lender — and a strategy to graduate back to A-lender pricing within 1–2 years.

body.heading: What scores mean for your options
paragraphs: 680+: full A-lender access at best rates. 640–680: most A-lenders will still fund, possibly at slightly worse pricing. 600–640: B-lender territory (Home Trust, Equitable, MCAP B-side). Rates typically 1–2% above A-lender. 550–600: B-lender or credit union, usually with stronger down payment (20%+). Below 550: private lender financing, short term, expensive (8–12%+) — used as a 12–18 month bridge to a refinance back into B or A.
Almost any credit situation has a path forward. The question is what the bridge looks like and how quickly you can graduate to better pricing.

body.heading: The 1–2 year rebuild plan
paragraphs: Most clients in the 580–640 range can graduate to A-lender pricing within 24 months by: paying every credit obligation on time without exception, reducing utilization to under 30% of limits, settling any collections, and keeping credit accounts open (length of credit history matters). I track this with clients between renewal dates so the move back to A-lender pricing happens at the right moment.

whoItsFor: Have an Equifax score in the 540–680 range
Have past collections or a discharged consumer proposal/bankruptcy
Need to buy or refinance and don't qualify with A-lenders today

faqs.question: How long after a discharged bankruptcy can I get a mortgage?
faqs.answer: B-lenders: typically 1 year post-discharge with re-established credit. A-lenders: usually 2 years post-discharge, established R1/I1 credit on at least two trade lines.

faqs.question: What if I'm in a consumer proposal?
faqs.answer: B-lenders can fund mortgages during an active proposal in some cases (especially purchase money to pay it off). After discharge, options open up significantly.

faqs.question: Will my rate be terrible?
faqs.answer: Higher than prime, yes. The plan is to use B-lender financing as a bridge — fix the credit, build payment history, and refinance to A-lender pricing in 12–24 months.
